name: GitHub
description: Manage GitHub repos, issues, PRs, releases, and actions using the gh CLI. Agents get structured access to your repositories.
contextSnippet:
## GitHub (gh CLI)

The GitHub skill is enabled. Use `gh` CLI for all GitHub operations.

### Common commands
- `gh issue list/create/view/edit/close`
- `gh pr list/create/view/merge/checks`
- `gh release list/create/upload/delete`
- `gh run list/view/watch`
- `gh api <endpoint>` for any REST/GraphQL call

### Rules
- Always use `--json` flag for structured output (e.g. `gh issue list --json number,title,state`)
- Default repo: {GITHUB_DEFAULT_REPO}
- Use `gh auth status` to verify authentication

name: Vercel CLI
description: Deploy, manage, and monitor Vercel projects from the command line. Handle deployments, domains, environment variables, and more.

settings.label: Vercel Token
settings.help: Create a token at https://vercel.com/account/tokens. Required for CI/CD and non-interactive use.

settings.label: Vercel Org ID
settings.help: Optional. Found in .vercel/project.json after running vercel link.

settings.label: Vercel Project ID
settings.help: Optional. Found in .vercel/project.json after running vercel link.

contextSnippet:
## Vercel CLI

The Vercel skill is enabled. Use `vercel` CLI for all Vercel operations.

### Deploying
- `vercel` or `vercel deploy` - deploy to preview
- `vercel deploy --prod` - deploy to production
- `vercel redeploy [deployment-url]` - rebuild and redeploy

### Project Management
- `vercel link` - link local directory to a Vercel project
- `vercel pull` - pull remote env vars and project settings
- `vercel build` - build project locally
- `vercel dev` - run development server locally
- `vercel project ls/add/rm` - manage projects

### Deployments
- `vercel list` - list recent deployments
- `vercel inspect [deployment-url]` - get deployment details
- `vercel logs [deployment-url]` - view runtime logs (add --follow for streaming)
- `vercel promote [deployment-url]` - promote a deployment to production
- `vercel rollback [deployment-url]` - roll back production to a previous deployment
- `vercel remove [deployment-url]` - remove a deployment

### Environment Variables
- `vercel env ls` - list env vars
- `vercel env add [name] [environment]` - add env var (production/preview/development)
- `vercel env rm [name] [environment]` - remove env var
- `vercel env pull [file]` - pull env vars to local .env file

### Domains & DNS
- `vercel domains ls/add/rm/buy` - manage domains
- `vercel dns ls/add/rm` - manage DNS records
- `vercel certs ls/issue/rm` - manage SSL certificates
- `vercel alias set [deployment] [domain]` - set custom domain alias

### Teams & Auth
- `vercel login` - authenticate with Vercel
- `vercel whoami` - show current user
- `vercel switch [team]` - switch team scope
- `vercel teams list/add/invite` - manage teams

### Rules
- Use `--token $VERCEL_TOKEN` for non-interactive/CI usage
- Use `--yes` flag to skip confirmation prompts in automation
- Use `--prod` flag to target production environment
- Use `vercel whoami` to verify authentication

name: Figma Design
description: Access Figma designs, components, and design tokens. Read design files, inspect properties, extract assets, and get design specifications.
contextSnippet:
## Figma Design (MCP)

The Figma skill is enabled via remote MCP server. Authentication is handled via OAuth browser flow.

### Available Operations
- Read Figma design files and inspect layer hierarchy
- Get component properties, styles, and design tokens
- Extract text content, colors, spacing, and typography
- Inspect auto-layout and constraint settings
- Get export/asset URLs for design elements

### Usage Notes
- First use will prompt OAuth authentication in the browser
- No API key needed - auth is handled automatically
- Use Figma file URLs or node IDs to reference specific designs
- Design data is read-only - modifications must be made in Figma directly
